steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Identify the decisive clue "far more than simply concealing his authorship" which signals that the blank should introduce a statement that emphasizes or reinforces the pseudonym's additional purpose.

The logical relationship is emphatic affirmation. The sentence establishes that the pseudonym did "far more than simply concealing authorship," and the blank introduces the elaboration that proves it.
  • Choice A — "Conversely,": signals contrast or opposition — the second sentence agrees with and extends the first, not contradicts it, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "In addition,": signals an extra separate point, but the second sentence explains the "far more" already claimed — it reinforces rather than adds, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "However,": signals an unexpected reversal, but the second sentence amplifies the claim rather than contradicting it, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "Indeed,": signals emphatic confirmation of the "far more" claim, introducing the evidence that proves it, so fits. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
